central_data_reality: No single public dataset was identified that combines breast-cancer treatment cycles, CBC trends, symptoms, medications, MRI response, CT/ultrasound metastatic indicators, clinician notes, and outcomes.
recommended_strategy: Use a source-calibrated synthetic patient journey benchmark: real public MRI-response datasets for imaging signals, QIN-BREAST/FDG-PET-CT-Lesions/DeepLesion for CT/PET-CT workflow experiments, BUSI/BUS-UCLM for breast ultrasound segmentation experiments, MIMIC-IV for lab-distribution realism, SEER/TCGA/METABRIC for subtype and outcome priors, and explicit documentation for all synthetic fields.

sources.name: I-SPY2 Breast MRI Collection
sources.access: public TCIA collection; follow TCIA data citation and use terms
limitations: Imaging-response focused; does not provide a full patient monitoring journey.
Does not provide longitudinal CBC or patient-reported symptom data.

sources.name: Duke Breast Cancer MRI
sources.access: public TCIA collection; follow TCIA data citation and use terms
limitations: Primarily pre-operative MRI; not a complete treatment timeline.
Response labels are not available for every patient.

sources.name: BreastDCEDL
sources.access: public research dataset/paper; use source-specific access instructions
use_in_project: External validation direction for response modeling
Feature distribution comparison against synthetic journeys
limitations: Strong for DCE-MRI response modeling, weak for daily clinical monitoring.
Does not include CBC, medication administration, or patient-reported symptoms.

sources.name: MIMIC-IV
limitations: Not breast-cancer treatment specific.
Best used for lab-distribution realism, not oncology response labels.

sources.name: SEER Research Data
sources.access: public-use research data with SEER data-use agreement
limitations: Registry data, not a patient monitoring timeline.
No CBC trajectories, imaging series, or free-text symptom stream.

sources.name: TCGA-BRCA / METABRIC via cBioPortal
sources.access: public cBioPortal exploration/download where permitted by study
limitations: Useful for priors, not for treatment-cycle monitoring.
No CBC nadirs, patient chat symptoms, or serial response imaging workflow.

sources.name: QIN-BREAST
sources.access: public TCIA collection; follow TCIA data citation and use terms
use_in_project: Longitudinal PET/CT and MRI workflow exploration
Future external-direction testing for response monitoring
limitations: Imaging-focused; does not include CBC, patient symptoms, or full care-team workflow data.
Useful for narrow imaging experiments, not direct clinical deployment.

sources.name: FDG-PET-CT-Lesions
sources.access: public TCIA collection; follow TCIA data citation and use terms
use_in_project: PET/CT DICOM ingestion and mask-handling tests
limitations: Not breast-cancer specific.
Lesion segmentation labels do not by themselves establish metastatic breast cancer origin.

sources.name: DeepLesion
sources.access: public NIH-hosted dataset; check NIH use/citation guidance
use_in_project: Future CT lesion detection pretraining or benchmarking
Stress testing CT image ingestion separate from breast-specific claims
limitations: Not cancer-type specific and not a breast cancer treatment-response dataset.
Bounding boxes identify lesions, not clinical diagnosis or disease origin.

sources.name: BUSI - Breast Ultrasound Images Dataset
sources.access: public mirror; verify original paper citation and mirror license before redistribution
use_in_project: Benign/malignant label handling experiments with strict non-diagnostic language
limitations: Breast lesion dataset only; does not cover abdominal ascites or metastatic spread.
Small and commonly re-hosted; requires careful split and duplicate checks.

sources.name: BUS-UCLM Breast Ultrasound Lesion Segmentation
sources.access: public dataset described in Scientific Data; follow article/data repository terms
limitations: Breast ultrasound only; not a metastatic or ascites dataset.
Should be used for segmentation/robustness experiments, not diagnosis claims.

feature_feasibility.project_action: Use for external response-label direction and imaging feature calibration.
Use EHR lab distributions to tune synthetic missingness, units, timing jitter, and outliers.
Keep synthetic symptom generation, calibrate severity rates from trial adverse-event tables where available.
Use public trial schemas and guideline/paper schedules as priors, not as patient-level ground truth.
Use for DICOM ingestion, whole-body lesion workflow experiments, and non-diagnostic clinician-review signals.
Use for future breast ultrasound segmentation baselines with careful split, duplicate, and non-diagnostic caveats.
Implement report-text extraction now; add image tasks only after ascites/peritoneal labels are curated or annotated.
Use source-calibrated synthetic journeys and clearly disclose the limitation.

claim_boundary: These sources improve realism and external-direction testing, but they do not make the system clinically validated or diagnostic.
